summary: Verification of Accountant → Cashbook → Direct Expense flow. Backend: 5/5 pytest cases pass for /api/accountant/cashbook-filtered and /api/accountant/overview. Frontend: all 3 sub-tabs (Material/Labour/Petty Cash) render with correct data-testids, correct accent colors (blue/purple/amber) when active, and correct live count+total chips. Material is default-active. Table filters correctly per sub-tab. CashbookDateFilter renders ABOVE sub-tabs with Month/Year defaults (Apr/2026 = current), Meta-style popover opens with all 9 presets (Today, Yesterday, This Week, Last 7 Days, This Month, Last Month, Last 30 Days, Last 90 Days, Clear). Changing Month (Apr→Mar) triggers refetch (Material pill changes from '5 · ₹0' to '6 · ₹79,000', back to '5 · ₹0' on Apr). Project filter present above sub-tabs. Add Expense opens inline add row with category/payment/amount/vendor/project fields. Seed data confirmed: 5 material expenses on Swathi 60L G+2 in Apr 2026.

minor.issue: Approved-material-request expense entries expose 'payment_type' instead of 'payment_method'. UI falls back to 'Cash'. Consider normalising to payment_method in the serialiser for consistency with manually-added expenses.

The 5 April 2026 material expenses all have amount=0. Not a code bug (stages awaiting priced POs), but end-users may find the chip 'Material 5 · ₹0' confusing. Consider excluding 0-amount entries from the chip total or adding a tooltip.

action_items: (Optional/Minor) Normalise expense_entries shape: expose payment_method on approved-material-request entries (currently only payment_type).
(Optional) Surface a tooltip on pill chip when count>0 but total=0 to clarify amounts are pending.
critical_code_review_comments: CashbookTab correctly defaults dateFrom/dateTo to current month start/end and useCallback deps are correct ([dateFrom, dateTo, filterProject]).
filteredExpenses uses strict equality on expense_type === 'material' | 'labour' | 'petty_cash' — matches backend values returned by the endpoint.
data-testid coverage is excellent on the three sub-tabs, date-picker, month/year selects, project filter, and add-expense button — easy to automate.
CashbookDateFilter uses correct monthBoundsISO helper; deduceMonthYear correctly reflects current month when ranges align.
test_credentials: admin@constructionos.com / Demo@1234 (Demo Access > Super Admin tile works; direct email+password login via the form returned 401 in the preview env — Demo Access path used).
seed_data_creation: No new data created. Verified existing seed: 5 material expenses on project 'Swathi 60L G+2' in April 2026.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The Cashbook Direct Expense flow is fully verified. Backend: session-cookie auth via POST /api/auth/login (no JWT). Use requests.Session() + cookie 'session_token'. Key data-testids: cashbook-date-picker-btn, cashbook-month-select, cashbook-year-select, cashbook-project-filter, cashbook-preset-<name>, expense-filter-material|labour|petty_cash, add-expense-btn, submit-expense-row-btn, cashbook-expense-table. Direct email/password login on the form returns 401 in preview — use Demo Access > Super Admin tile instead.
observations: Frontend login form at /login rejects email/password with 401 for admin@constructionos.com/Demo@1234 even though POST /api/auth/login with same creds returns 200 via curl. The Demo Access flow works. Minor, but E1 may want to verify the login form's request body/endpoint.
Backend test skipped in first run because login response has no JSON 'token' field (session-cookie auth); fixture updated to use cookie-based auth.
